title: [compat API] unable to prune images with a dangling filter
body:
### Issue Description

I'm unable to use the compat API (docker API) with Podman for the pruneImage command
https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.42/#tag/Image/operation/ImagePrune



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. pull an image (assuming it's not already used/there before)
2. ```
   podman pull quay.io/podman/hello
   ```
3. call the REST API


```shell
curl -X POST --unix-socket /var/run/docker.sock "http:/v1.41/images/prune?filters=\{\"dangling\":\{\"false\":true\}\}" 
```


### Describe the results you received

I got
```
{"cause":"specifying \"dangling\" filter more than once with different values is not supported","message":"specifying \"dangling\" filter more than once with different values is not supported","response":500}
```




### Describe the results you expected

I should receive something like:

```
{"ImagesDeleted":[{"Untagged":"quay.io/podman/hello:latest"},....
```

### podman info output

```yaml
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.4.1
API Version:  4.4.1
Go Version:   go1.19
Git Commit:   34e8f3933242f2e566bbbbf343cf69b7d506c1cf
Built:        Wed Feb  8 23:21:26 2023
OS/Arch:      darwin/amd64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.4.1
API Version:  4.4.1
Go Version:   go1.19.5
Built:        Thu Feb  9 11:58:53 2023
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
@vrothberg PTAL

Problems seems to be that podman adds the dangling filter here: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/a54a4041c5d5094c5925f2be4ef09a383c16f53b/pkg/domain/infra/abi/images.go#L58
and thus validation in libimage complains about it.

This can also be reproduced with `podman image prune -f --filter dangling=false`
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Closing as this has been fixed in newer versions.
```
gvyas-mac:dangling-image gvyas$ podman image prune --filter dangling=false
WARNING! This command removes all dangling images.
Are you sure you want to continue? [y/N] y
42c841a7c583fc861b012c04b6c8ca8ec721228b851fde89159cf730d3836299
```

@vyasgun: You can't close an active issue/PR unless you authored it or you are a collaborator.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17614#issuecomment-1494333081):

>/close


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
@vrothberg is it possible to grant collaborator access to @vyasgun ?

> Closing as this has been fixed in newer versions.

Seems this isn't fixed as of the latest 4.5.0 - in what version has this been released?
